habitat: Forests; woodlands; savanna; shrubland; plantations; and urban greenspaces
story: The African Crowned Eagle (Stephanoaetus coronatus) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Accipitridae family. It is most commonly observed in Forests; woodlands; savanna; shrubland; plantations; and urban greenspaces, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Near Threatened (NT) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the African Crowned Eagle is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the African Crowned Eagle into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
